_comment: External-tool registry (AI_OPERATIONS_PLAN §2.3 standing rule): every external CLI or MCP tool must have an entry here BEFORE any integration. Human-readable companion: docs/tool-registry.md. This file is DECLARATIVE POLICY ONLY — nothing reads it mechanically yet; enforcement wiring (permission allowlists, guard rules, verify checks) ships with the first enabled lane as its own Tier-C reviewed feature. This is NOT a model policy: Claude Code sub-agent model assignment lives exclusively in model-policy.json (tiers/agents) and external vendor models must never appear there.
_integration_pattern: Any enabled tool runs as a sandboxed one-shot LEAF WORKER invoked via Bash: single briefed task, output captured under roadmap/evidence/, then re-validated by this repo's own verify gate and a fresh-context evaluator. Never a peer orchestrator; never spawning agents; its native review/subagent modes are NOT a substitute for this repo's evaluator (2026-07-17 research pass: Codex's own docs state there is 'no separate verification or judge role distinct from workers').
_trust_levels: untrusted-output — the only level currently defined: everything the tool produces is treated as an untrusted contribution and takes the same review path as external code (gate + evaluator + security-reviewer on sensitive paths).
_secrets_policy: Env-var NAMES only, never values (CLAUDE.md §7). Subscription/OAuth login is the preferred auth everywhere it exists (plan §7.2 subscription-first); API-key envs are documented fallbacks, never requirements.
_status: Registry active since 2026-07-17. ZERO tools enabled. Every entry: status=candidate, approval_gate=operator (roadmap/QUESTIONS.md Q-0005). Confidence tags from the 2026-07-17 adversarially-verified research pass (92 claims, 25 vote-tested: 18 confirmed / 7 killed), updated by the 2026-07-18 frontier-delta pass (10 claim bundles x 3 refuters, 30 agents) — durable findings in docs/FRONTIER.md §4; per-tool detail in docs/tool-registry.md.

codex-cli.product: Codex CLI — github.com/openai/codex (Apache-2.0, ~99k stars, re-verified via GitHub API in the research pass)
codex-cli.purpose: Leaf-worker lane for one-shot briefed implementation or heterogeneous second-opinion passes, run headlessly via `codex exec`; output re-validated by this repo's gate + evaluator.

auth.subscription: codex login (ChatGPT-plan OAuth, browser flow); device-code beta `codex login --device-auth` for browser-less headless runs. CONFIRMED against primary docs: ChatGPT-login and API-key are two co-equal paths — the 'OAuth-first with API-key fallback' hierarchy claim was adversarially REFUTED. CONFIRMED 2026-07-18 (3-vote, learn.chatgpt.com/docs/pricing): ChatGPT Plus and Pro plans include 'Codex on the web, in the CLI, in the IDE extension, and on iOS' with published 5-hour-window message ranges (Plus: Sol 15-90 / Terra 20-110 / Luna 50-280; Pro 5x/20x higher); 'Additional weekly limits may apply', no figures stated.

allowed_commands: codex exec <briefed one-shot task> — non-interactive only; sandbox_mode=workspace-write; network access disabled; danger-full-access banned under this registry
codex-cli.network: None for leaf work. Codex's own sandbox defaults network OFF (CONFIRMED, primary docs; caveat: some 'managed allowlist' language in sources describes ChatGPT cloud Work-mode, a distinct mechanism from the local CLI sandbox).
codex-cli.sandbox: Vendor-provided, most mature of the three (CONFIRMED across ~8 adversarial votes): read-only / workspace-write / danger-full-access modes; OS-native enforcement (Seatbelt on macOS, bubblewrap on Linux/WSL2, native Windows sandbox); declarative config.toml.
codex-cli.approval_gate: operator — Q-0005 before first live invocation; any wiring change is Tier C (security-reviewer mandatory)
codex-cli.confidence: Most-verified ecosystem of the three (sandboxing + auth confirmed against primary docs by multiple adversarial votes). Lowest-risk first candidate if a lane is ever funded.
open_questions: RESOLVED 2026-07-18: MCP both directions primary-confirmed — client (`codex mcp add/list/get/remove`) and server (`codex mcp-server`, 'Run Codex as an MCP server over stdio'); the CLI reference labels the server Stable while the in-repo protocol doc says experimental — discrepancy carried, not resolved
KILLED claim — do not rely on: Ultra-mode subagents as a builder/evaluator analog; they are homogeneous parallel workers with no judge role

Known vendor limitation, irrelevant to this repo's leaf-worker doctrine (we never use external subagents) but recorded so nobody re-buys it: under GPT-5.6 Sol's MultiAgent V2, `hide_spawn_agent_metadata=true` strips model/agent_type fields from spawn_agent — a Sol parent cannot pin Terra/Luna subagents (openai/codex #31814, sibling #31882, live mid-July 2026)

grok-build.product: Grok Build — github.com/xai-org/grok-build (open-sourced ~2026-07-14/15); default model Grok 4.5
grok-build.status: candidate (NOT enabled) — pricing-tier blocker resolved 2026-07-18; remaining open items: free-tier semantics + Windows sandbox coverage
grok-build.purpose: Leaf-worker lane candidate: headless `grok -p` with streaming-json output; same capture-and-revalidate pattern.

auth.subscription: Browser OAuth on first launch (CONFIRMED, primary docs). Tier-gate RESOLVED 2026-07-18 against primary: x.ai/pricing's comparison matrix marks Grok Build included on ALL six plans (Free, SuperGrok Lite, SuperGrok $30/mo, SuperGrok Heavy, Business, Enterprise) and x.ai/cli states verbatim 'Available to try for Free' (real-browser DOM + curl-with-browser-UA; plain automated fetch is bot-blocked). The old $30-$300 claims described rate/limit tiers, not a hard access gate. NOT STATED on primary pages: whether free access is standing or a ~7-day trial (secondary coverage says trial), and per-tier rate limits — carried UNVERIFIED.

allowed_commands: grok -p <briefed one-shot task> --output-format streaming-json — non-interactive only
grok-build.network: Executes locally; 'only API calls to xAI leave your system' (UNTESTED-strong, non-primary). Vendor sandbox can block child-process network via seccomp (Linux) per 18-sandbox.md.
grok-build.sandbox: SUPERSEDED 2026-07-18 (3-vote, primary): user-guide 18-sandbox.md documents OS-level filesystem+network isolation — Landlock (+optional bubblewrap) on Linux (kernel >=5.13), Seatbelt on macOS, seccomp child-network block; five profiles off/workspace/devbox/read-only/strict; applied to the whole grok process at startup, irreversible; ~/.grok/sandbox.toml deny-lists kernel-enforced. WINDOWS NOT MENTIONED in the sandbox doc — and this repo runs Windows, so for a local lane isolation remains THIS repo's responsibility (optional-modules 'Native sandboxing' row); the vendor sandbox would cover a Linux-CI lane only.
grok-build.approval_gate: operator — Q-0005 before first live invocation; any wiring change is Tier C (security-reviewer mandatory)
grok-build.confidence: Product real and extensible (CONFIRMED: TUI + headless CLI + ACP; `grok inspect` documents config sources/instructions/skills/plugins/hooks/MCP); vendor sandbox now documented (Linux/macOS only); access not tier-gated per primary. Repo active (three monorepo syncs 2026-07-16/17, incl. an SSRF fix in the hook runner) but NO version tags/releases published yet — no stable-release anchor to pin.
open_questions: Free-tier semantics: standing access vs ~7-day trial, and per-tier rate limits — not stated on primary pages (secondary coverage says trial); check on first login
Windows sandbox coverage: 18-sandbox.md names Linux/macOS mechanisms only; Windows prebuilt binaries exist but sandboxing there is not documented

KILLED 2026-07-18 (3-0 against primary) — do not re-cite: '8 concurrent worktree subagents' and 'Arena Mode'; 16-subagents.md documents spawn_subagent with isolation none|worktree and 'the maximum nesting depth is one', no numeric concurrency limit, no Arena

antigravity-cli.product: Antigravity CLI `agy` (launched ~2026-05-19 at I/O 2026; Google's stated successor to Gemini CLI — refined 2026-07-17: Gemini CLI's HOSTED serving for consumer AI Pro/Ultra stopped 2026-06-18, but the OSS repo is NOT archived, v0.51.0 released 2026-07-16)
antigravity-cli.status: candidate (NOT enabled) — headless question RESOLVED 2026-07-18 (mode exists); remaining blocker is auth (no API-key path) + docs-site silence on headless
antigravity-cli.purpose: Forward-looking Google lane target. Leaf-worker eligibility CORRECTED 2026-07-18: headless `-p`/`--print` EXISTS — the CLI's own CHANGELOG names it 'headless' with an active fix history (v1.0.8 fixed the Windows non-TTY stdout drop; flagship bug #76 closed 2026-07-12 via ~1.1.1) — but the docs site documents only the interactive TUI, and unattended runs would ride a cached OAuth session (untested).

auth.subscription: Browser Google Sign-In, token cached in the OS keyring — Apple Keychain / Linux secret-service / Windows Credential Manager (CONFIRMED 2026-07-17, docs + repo README); print mode supports pasting the OAuth authorization code (CHANGELOG v1.0.11). Individual tier $0/mo (agent models incl. Gemini 3.5 Flash, Gemini 3.1 Pro, Claude Sonnet & Opus 4.6, gpt-oss-120b); AI Ultra $100/mo = 5x and $200/mo = 20x Antigravity usage vs Pro (blog.google 2026-05-19). Google Cloud MCP surfaces authenticate via Application Default Credentials / short-lived service-account impersonation, not bare API keys (CONFIRMED for the Data Agent Kit extension specifically).
auth.headless_fallback_env: none — API-key auth explicitly unsupported: maintainer verbatim 2026-07 (#78, open): 'Gemini API Key is not supported currently... For using an API key in Antigravity you can use the SDK.' Google's recommended CI path is the separate Antigravity SDK (Python, github.com/google-antigravity/antigravity-sdk-python) — a different surface this registry has not evaluated

antigravity-cli.sandbox: The separate Gemini API 'Managed Agents' surface has a CONFIRMED isolated Linux sandbox (Preview/pre-GA); Antigravity docs' live-render describes a terminal sandbox using 'AppContainer on Windows' (single-source, UNTESTED — the docs site is a JS SPA that defeats plain fetchers). Minimum Windows 10 64-bit.
antigravity-cli.approval_gate: operator — Q-0005; plus a smoke-test of headless `-p` on a cached OAuth session before any wiring design
antigravity-cli.confidence: Still lowest of the three, but improved 2026-07-18: headless mode is real (changelog-evidenced) and actively bug-fixed; auth remains OAuth-only (a structural mismatch with unattended CI), and docs-site coverage of headless is absent — the marketing-vs-docs gap persists.
open_questions: Unattended-run viability on a cached keyring OAuth session: untested; token lifetime/refresh behavior not stated anywhere examined
Antigravity SDK (Python) as the Google-recommended CI path: a separate surface, not yet evaluated by this registry
Claimed 5-event lifecycle-hooks architecture + subagent primitives: single blog source; Google's primary docs examined in the pass are silent on hooks/subagents (UNTESTED-weak)

KILLED claims — do not rely on: 'working multi-agent orchestration for real coding tasks' and 'closest analog to this repo's subagent/hook architecture'; both refuted 2026-07-17, no rehabilitating evidence found since
sources: Google developer blog + trade press (I/O 2026 launch)
